subject: refactor(markdown): replace regex parser with marked library
body:
The hand-rolled regex markdown parser had fundamental structural bugs:
multi-line code blocks broken by the line-by-line paragraph regex,
double-escaping of already-escaped content, and raw < characters
creating broken HTML elements that truncated rendering.

Replace with marked (v17, ~43KB bundled/13KB gzipped) configured with
a custom renderer for DaisyUI styling:
- Code blocks: copy button wrapper, manual escapeHtml
- Inline code: custom badge-like classes
- Tables: scrollable overflow wrapper, parseInline for cell tokens
- Links: URL validation (allowlist http/https/relative), link-primary class
- Bold: font-semibold class
- Checkboxes: DaisyUI checkbox-xs styling
- Everything else: marked defaults (headings, paragraphs, blockquotes, lists)

Migration from classic scripts to ES modules:
- public/markdown.js (classic IIFE) -> src/markdown.js (ES module)
- Vite bundles marked as a dependency
- HTML script tags updated to type="module" (deferred, execute in order)
- Inline page scripts also type="module" so they run after markdown.js

Verified: 42 markdown files render correctly, 0 double-escaped entities,
all code block counts match, all files render to completion.

Tags: markdown, refactor, marked, dependencies
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: markdown-ux
Semver: minor
